column1: # The CURATED list of composite clades to report (Script 005, composite_clades).
# A composite clade is a focused question about where an annogroup's member species
# fall on the species tree. One composite clade per row; four columns (tab-separated):
#   Name         : short name for this composite clade. LEAVE BLANK for exact and
#                  absent (they do not vary for a given clade set, so they are
#                  auto-named from their clades); REQUIRED for core_urclade /
#                  core_early_clade (which vary by target and threshold).
#   Target_Clade : the focal clade for core_urclade / core_early_clade. BLANK for
#   Clades       : comma-delimited clade names — the COMPONENTS (exact), the clades
#                  the members must be ABSENT from (absent), or the INGROUPS
# Clade names resolve to species sets via the `composite_clades` block of
# START_HERE-user_config.yaml: a building-block group name (Ctenophora, Porifera,
# Placozoa, Cnidaria, Bilateria), the outside label (NonMetazoa, exact only), the
# scope name (Metazoa), or any raw clade_id_name (e.g. C082_Metazoa).
# The algorithms (member species = the species an annogroup's sequences come from):
#   exact            : members come from BOTH/ALL and ONLY the listed components.
#   absent           : members are ABSENT from ALL listed clades. Auto-named
#                      cc_<clades>-absent; the outside label (NonMetazoa) is allowed
#   core_urclade     : members in >=1 OUTGROUP of the target AND >=1 listed ingroup
#   core_early_clade : members in >=2 listed ingroups, the ingroups being the
#                      ambiguous (unresolved) nodes that define the target's "Early"
# absent / core_urclade / core_early_clade are independent tests, so one annogroup
# can match several. Blank and '#' lines are ignored; component order does not matter
# (exact re-sorts into config order). Edit freely.
# ---- exact: Bilateria, and the Porifera+Bilateria pair ----
# ---- exact: the same combinations, plus non-metazoan outgroup members ----
# ---- absent: members absent from all listed clades (auto-named cc_<clades>-absent) ----
# ---- core_urclade: target's Ur (LCA) core (in an outgroup AND an ingroup) ----
